title: FLIP Fitness Landscape Inference for Proteins Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Paperclip and the FLIP paper describe scripts/data as freely accessible through this benchmark site.
Use paper and supplementary materials to recover split semantics.

instructions: Use the FLIP benchmark site to select GB1, AAV, or thermostability rather than downloading unrelated assets.
Record task, split name, wild-type sequence, mutation notation, fitness column, and benchmark version.
Prefer provided biologically motivated splits as operating points, then construct SPECTRA curves over mutation and sequence support.

scale.download_size: lightweight to moderate by selected landscape; use one landscape first

recommended_axes.name: mutation_depth
recommended_axes.definition: Number of substitutions or edits separating a variant from the wild type and from training variants.

recommended_axes.name: mutation_position_support
recommended_axes.definition: Support for mutated positions or local sequence windows in the training set.

recommended_axes.name: sequence_identity
recommended_axes.definition: Protein sequence identity or edit distance to nearest training variants.

recommended_axes.name: fitness_extrapolation
recommended_axes.definition: High-fitness or low-fitness target support; diagnostic unless based only on training labels for split construction.

useful_for: Testing protein fitness extrapolation beyond random variant splits.
Comparing random, mutational-depth, position, and cluster operating points.
Lightweight sequence-fitness SPECTRA examples complementary to ProteinGym.
leakage_risks: Variants from the same combinatorial library can leak position and mutational context.
Fitness-derived axes are post-hoc if held-out labels are used.
Downsampling nonfunctional variants can change the deployment distribution.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip identified FLIP as a protein fitness benchmark with GB1, AAV, and thermostability tasks and extrapolative split settings.
